government representative, usually called agent superintendent) assigned each agency.

DARTING around Valley home back patio playing with trucks wouldn't think Jack Stokes almost died last month.

Late last year Lara noticed Jack's right started deviate outwards, when visited told "just lazy eye".
Weeks later worsened husband Brock noticed slight glow Jack's pupil.
took Gladstone Hospital emergency department Years Day, after waiting five hours again told "it's just lazy eye".
took optometrist next day, thought something right," said.
Gladstone optometrist believed retinoblastoma, referred Jack ophthalmologist specialist Brisbane further test.
January Jack undergoing exploratory surgery Lady Cilento Children's Hospital Brisbane, which confirmed tumour taken over third right eye.
two-year-old surgery remove cancerous eye.
"The doctor told life-threatening situation, that could've lost life," Lara said.
half knew that already with what read (about disease), it's quite shock actually hear it."
two-year-old still more tests next week cancer spread.
taken doctors' word could've cost life," said.
health scare comes time when this family needed reminders cruel life
March last year Lara diagnosed with tongue cancer, which still recovering from.
idea losing young Jack haunting couple, after Lara miscarriages before born.
"Nothing prepare something like (losing your child)," said.
think could've lost him, he's most beautiful little world, know might think bias, he's such gentle spirited boy.
"You hear stories about this happening never think could happen you."
Jack's case being investigated Gladstone Hospital they keeping touch with Lara about their findings.
case "mother's intuition" that likely real saviour.
Currently Jack donor without pupil iris.
will help skull develop properly, since he's still toddler.
he's taken ordeal stride, returning usual playful, adventurous mood soon stepped foot onto their Valley home back patio.
"He's actually perfect
He's exceptional," Lara said.
